title_en: Texas is waging a battle to stop “Islamification”
title_zh: 德克萨斯正发起一场阻止“伊斯兰化”的斗争
rubric_en: America’s biggest Republican state offers a playbook for a new identity politics
rubric_zh: 美国最大的共和党州为新的身份政治提供了样本

paragraphs.en: Eighteen months ago Republicans in Texas took on a new fight. It began in February 2025, when Greg Abbott, the governor, described a housing development proposed by the East Plano Islamic Centre as an example of “sharia cities”. A dozen state agencies then launched investigations. In November he designated the Council on American-Islamic Relations (CAIR), America’s biggest Muslim civil-rights group, a “foreign terrorist organisation” and barred it from buying land in the state. In March the state comptroller shut Muslim schools out of Texas’s new voucher scheme. The state Republican Party now ranks “Stop the Islamification of Texas” second on its list of legislative priorities.
paragraphs.zh: 18个月前，德克萨斯州的共和党人开启了一场新的斗争。它始于2025年2月，当时州长格雷格·阿博特（Greg Abbott）将东帕洛伊斯兰中心（East Plano Islamic Centre）提出的一项住房开发项目描述为“伊斯兰法城（sharia cities）”的一个例子。随后，州内十二个州级机构发起了调查。11月，他将美国最大的穆斯林民权组织——美洲伊斯兰关系委员会（CAIR）定为“外国恐怖组织”，并禁止其在州内购买土地。今年3月，州财政主管将穆斯林学校排除在德州新的学费券计划之外。州共和党现在将“阻止德州伊斯兰化”列为其立法优先事项名单中的第二位。

paragraphs.en: On its face, this looks like a revival of the backlash to radical Islam that followed the 9/11 attacks. But today’s campaign is driven by fear not so much of external violence as of internal transformation. Across the country, support is swelling for ideas that fuse American civic life with Christian values, loosely defined as Christian nationalism. How this agenda will evolve is still unclear. But Texas, America’s largest Republican state, is now showing one way forward, asserting America’s Christian roots while battling fiercely against a perceived threat.
paragraphs.zh: 从表面看，这似乎是对9·11袭击后反激进伊斯兰浪潮的卷土重来。但当下的运动驱动的更多是对外部暴力的恐惧，不如说是对内部变革的恐惧。全国范围内，对将美国公民社会与基督教价值观融合起来的理念——即所谓“基督教民族主义”——支持正在上升。这一议程将如何发展仍不明朗。但美国最大的共和党州——德克萨斯正在展示一条可能的路径，在捍卫美国基督教根基的同时，对被认为的威胁进行强硬反击。

paragraphs.en: “The United States of America was founded as a Christian nation,” Mr Abbott stated earlier this year, as part of a defence of religious freedom. Other prominent proponents of America’s Christian identity include J.D. Vance, the vice-president, Pete Hegseth, the secretary of war, and Charlie Kirk, a conservative influencer assassinated in 2025. Kirk, to whom Donald Trump posthumously awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom, tied America’s Christian character to a specific composition of citizenry. America, he argued, was living through a “constitutional crisis” because “you cannot have liberty if you do not have a Christian population”.
paragraphs.zh: 阿博特今年早些时候在一场捍卫宗教自由的表态中说过：“美利坚合众国是一个基督教国家。”美国基督教身份的其他主要倡导者包括副总统J·D·范斯（J.D. Vance）、国防部长皮特·赫格塞斯（Pete Hegseth）和于2025年被刺杀的保守派意见领袖查理·柯克（Charlie Kirk）。特朗普（Donald Trump）追授“总统自由勋章”给柯克。柯克把美国的基督教特质与特定的国民构成直接关联起来。他认为，美国正在经历一场“宪政危机”，因为“没有基督徒人口就不可能有自由”。

paragraphs.en: A growing number of politicians view Islam, in particular, as a threat to the country’s identity. In the 13 months to March, 46 Republican elected officials across America published 1,111 anti-Muslim posts, a 15-fold increase in monthly volume, according to the Centre for the Study of Organised Hate, a watchdog group. Many painted Islam as a death cult working to indoctrinate children and take over society. Tommy Tuberville, a senator from Alabama, shared a post that paired photos of New York’s Muslim mayor with the burning twin towers, warning that “the enemy is inside the gates.” Andy Ogles, a Tennessee congressman, said that “Muslims don’t belong in American society.”
paragraphs.zh: 越来越多的政治人物，尤其是针对伊斯兰教，认为其是对国家身份的威胁。在截至今年3月的13个月里，美国46名当选共和党官员共发布了1111条反穆斯林帖子，按月统计增长了15倍，这一数据来自监测组织“有组织仇恨研究中心”（Centre for the Study of Organised Hate）。其中许多人将伊斯兰描绘为一个教唆儿童洗脑、并试图接管社会的“死亡教派”。艾尔巴马州的参议员汤米·塔贝尔（Tommy Tuberville）发布了一则帖文，用纽约穆斯林市长的照片与“世贸中心双子塔遭遇恐怖袭击后燃烧”的画面并置，警告说“敌人就在城门之内”。田纳西州国会议员安迪·奥格尔斯（Andy Ogles）则表示“穆斯林不属于美国社会”。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump likes to point to European cities as disastrous parables, with Paris and London “no longer recognisable” and the latter moving towards “sharia law”. Last year two Texas congressmen, Chip Roy and Keith Self, founded the “Sharia-free America Caucus” in Congress. It has since recruited 66 other members from 25 states.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生喜欢把欧洲城市当作灾难性寓言来说明问题，称巴黎和伦敦“不再像原来的样子”，后者更是正走向“伊斯兰教法”。去年，德州两名国会议员奇普·罗伊（Chip Roy）和基思·塞尔夫（Keith Self）在国会成立了“无夏里亚法（Sharia-free）美国党团”。此后，它已从25个州吸收了另外66名成员。

paragraphs.en: This reflects a spreading anxiety within pockets of the electorate. Three-quarters of Republicans think that American values and Islam are in conflict, according to the Public Religion Research Institute, compared with 30% of Democrats, a partisan gap that has doubled since 2011 (see chart). Those who support the idea of America as a Christian nation are even more likely to think Islam is a risk.
paragraphs.zh: 这反映出在选民中的某些群体里，焦虑情绪正在扩散。公共宗教研究所的调查显示，三分之四的共和党人认为美国价值观与伊斯兰教存在冲突，而民主党人中这一比例为30%，自2011年以来这种党派差距已经翻倍（见图表）。那些认为美国应当是基督教国家的人，更加倾向于把伊斯兰教视为风险。

paragraphs.en: With Muslims making up about 1% of the country’s population, fears of a takeover may seem misplaced. To the movement’s proponents, that isn’t the point. “Christian values form the foundation of our nation and the constitutional system that governs our daily lives,” noted Mr Self in a recent press release celebrating the success of the anti-sharia caucus. “Sharia and its principles”, he added, “stand in direct opposition to those foundations.” Muslims are the perfect foil for defining who is a “true citizen”, says Andrew Whitehead, who studies Christian nationalism at the University of Indiana.
paragraphs.zh: 穆斯林仅占全国人口约1%，所以对“被接管”的担忧似乎有些错位。对这场运动的支持者来说，这并非关键。塞尔夫最近在一份新闻稿中说，恭喜反夏里亚法党团取得成功时表示，“基督教价值观是我们国家的基石，也是支配我们日常生活的宪政体系的基础”。他又补充道，“夏里亚法及其原则”与这些基石“构成直接对立”。印第安纳大学研究基督教民族主义的安德鲁·怀特黑德（Andrew Whitehead）说，穆斯林是界定“真正公民”身份的完美参照物。

paragraphs.en: Congress has a few nascent bills. Mr Roy has introduced legislation to bar foreigners who observe sharia law from entering the country, and Randy Fine of Florida has filed a facetious one to “protect puppies” from sharia bans. Texas is a few steps ahead. Mr Abbott’s office told The Economist that the governor’s policies do not vilify “law-abiding Texas Muslims”, but target “those who aid terrorism or attempt to impose foreign law on American soil”.
paragraphs.zh: 国会里已有几项酝酿中的法案。罗伊提出立法，打算禁止那些遵守夏里亚法的外国人入境；佛罗里达州的兰迪·费恩（Randy Fine）则提交了一项讽刺性质的法案，声称要“保护小狗”免受夏里亚法禁令影响。德州则领先一步。奥博特（Greg Abbott）办公室告诉《经济学人》：州长的政策并非妖魔化“遵纪守法的德州穆斯林”，而是针对“那些帮助恐怖主义者，或企图在美国土地上强加外来法律的人”。

paragraphs.en: In practice, Republican ideas are wide-ranging, from the attempt to stop taxpayer funds going to any organisation that has “allegiance to a foreign legal system” to a push “to deport advocates of sharia law”. After the Texas legislature required the Ten Commandments to be displayed in classrooms last year, in late June the state board of education overhauled its curriculum. New guidelines drop a middle-school “World Cultures” class and replace high-school units that “explain the significant beliefs of Islam” with ones that “describe the teachings of jihad and how they led to the expansion of Islam and the conquest of Christian land”. Pupils will now be required to read more Bible stories.
paragraphs.zh: 在实践中，这些共和党主张覆盖面很广，从试图阻止纳税人资金流向任何“效忠外国法系”的组织，到推动“驱逐伊斯兰法倡导者”。去年德州立法机构要求在教室内悬挂《十诫》后，教育委员会在六月下旬全面重组了课程。新的教学指南取消了一个初中“世界文化”课程，并把高中里原本“解释伊斯兰重要信仰”的单元，改成“描述圣战教义及其如何促成伊斯兰扩张和对基督教土地的征服”。学生今后将被要求阅读更多《圣经》故事。

paragraphs.en: For Republicans who have defended religious liberty, this is awkward. Shaimaa Zayan of CAIR, the non-profit Mr Abbott branded a terrorist organisation, says what was once “Islamophobia” has now turned into “religious persecution”. “They’re trying to make our lives hell so we self-deport,” she contends. Sam Westrop of the Texas Public Policy Foundation argues that the “mob-like noise” on social media obscures a genuinely dangerous rise in Islamist extremism in America. The right, he says, needs to realise that ordinary Muslims could be allies in the fight against it.
paragraphs.zh: 对于一直以来捍卫宗教自由的共和党人来说，这显得十分尴尬。CAIR的Shaimaa Zayan——这家被Abbott先生指认为恐怖主义组织的非营利机构——表示，过去被称作“伊斯兰恐惧症”的事，现在已经变成了“宗教迫害”。她指出：“他们正试图让我们的生活变得地狱般，以便我们自己离开这里。”德州公共政策基金会的Sam Westrop则认为，社交媒体上的“乌合之众式噪音”掩盖了美国国内伊斯兰极端主义“真实且危险的上升”。他说，保守派需要意识到，普通穆斯林完全可能成为对抗这种极端主义的盟友。

paragraphs.en: When Oklahoma tried to quash consideration of sharia law by courts over a decade ago, a judge overturned the rule. Mr Abbott’s efforts in Texas have so far fared only slightly better. Courts have blocked the state’s attempt to exclude Islamic schools from the voucher programme and have allowed a religious-discrimination lawsuit brought by the East Plano Islamic Centre to proceed. A judge appointed by Ronald Reagan rebuked the state, observing that “no evidence has been presented” that the mosque intends to impose sharia law on Texans.
paragraphs.zh: 十多年前俄克拉何马州曾尝试通过法院否决对伊斯兰法的考量，但遭到法官推翻。Abbott先生在德州的努力到目前为止也只好一点点进展。法院已经阻止了州政府将伊斯兰学校排除在代金券项目之外的尝试，并允许由东普莱诺清真寺（East Plano Islamic Centre）提出的宗教歧视诉讼继续进行。一名由罗纳德·里根任命的法官斥责了州政府，并指出，“没有证据”表明该清真寺打算在德州推行伊斯兰法。

paragraphs.en: This is unlikely to deter Texan legislators, who are due to reconvene in January. They will take cues from Mr Abbott. “The governor will not allow empathy to enable bad actors who would destroy Texas and America from the inside, as has already happened in the United Kingdom,” says Andrew Mahaleris, his press secretary.
paragraphs.zh: 这不太可能吓退德州立法者，他们将在一月份重新开会，并会以Abbott先生的路线为样板。其新闻秘书Andrew Mahaleris表示：“州长不会允许“同情心”成为阻碍，借此让恶意行为者得逞；这些人会从内部摧毁德州和美国，正如这在英国已经发生过。”

paragraphs.en: Other states are taking note. Mr Fine, the Florida congressman, says a recent poll of Republican primary voters in his district found they cared seven times more about “Islam in America” than abortion, and six times more than about gun rights. When asked by The Economist to define “sharia law”, Mr Fine skirted the question, instead reiterating that Islam is rooted in an ideology of “conquest and submission” that threatens democracy. As for others who are pushing their religion into public life? The Jewish lawmaker is conciliatory. “The United States is a Christian nation,” he says. “We need to understand where we live.”■
paragraphs.zh: 其他州也在关注这一动向。佛莱姆州众议员法因表示，他所在选区内共和党初选选民的一项最新民意调查显示，他们对“美国的伊斯兰化”这一问题的关注度比堕胎高出七倍，也比枪支权利高出六倍。该福克斯媒体人在《经济学人》问及“伊斯兰教法”时没有正面回应，反而重申伊斯兰根基于一种“征服与屈从”的意识形态，威胁民主。至于那些把宗教带入公共生活的人呢？这位犹太籍立法者采取了和解的说法：“美国是一个基督教国家，”他说，“我们需要明白自己生活在什么样的国度里。”■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 关注美国政治请订阅《简报中的美国》——我们的每日通讯，快速解析最重要的政治新闻；以及《制衡》周刊，深入讨论美国民主现状和选民关心的议题。
